preFix.title: Maintenance routines that extend domestic appliance lifespan
preFix.subtitle: Descaling, filter cleaning, door seal care, and annual checks for washing machines, dishwashers, and fridges
preFix.excerpt: The average UK washing machine lasts 11 to 12 years; with regular maintenance, 15 to 18 years is achievable for a quality machine. The most common causes of early failure are blocked filters, door seal deterioration, and limescale buildup in the drum and pump. Most maintenance tasks take 10 to 20 minutes and use materials costing 2 to 5 pounds.
preFix.sourceNotes: Which? Appliance Survey 2023 on average lifespan by category. WRAP Electrical Product Sustainability Survey 2022. Repair Cafe Foundation data on most common domestic appliance failure modes.

content.text: Data from 300 Repair Cafe events in 2023 found that 57 percent of appliances brought for repair had faults from blocked filters. Scaling. Or worn seals. All are preventable with maintenance. Extending appliance life by 3 to 5 years avoids 200 to 400 kg of manufacturing CO2 equivalent per appliance. Part of a broader circular economy approach to household consumption. Planned obsolescence shortens the default life of cheaper appliances. Maintenance cannot compensate fully for poor build quality. But for mid-range appliances it is the difference between 8 and 15 years of service.
Monthly: clean the filter. A blocked filter prevents the pump from draining fully and strains the pump motor. Remove, rinse, and replace.
Monthly: pull out the detergent drawer and rinse it thoroughly. Mould in the drawer contaminates the drum and accelerates deterioration of the rubber door seal.

Every 3 months: run a 90 degree cotton cycle with 250 ml of white vinegar in the drum. This descales the drum, heater element, and pipes. In hard water areas above 200 mg/l hardness. Which includes London and much of the South East, do this monthly.
Annually: check the door seal for cracks or deep mould. A cracked seal allows water to drip onto the motor. Replace the seal within 2 to 3 months of detecting cracks.
Monthly: remove and clean the filter at the bottom of the machine. A blocked filter causes poor cleaning results and strains the pump.
Every 3 months: remove the spray arms and clear the holes with a cocktail stick or pin. Blocked spray arm holes are the most common cause of poor wash performance in machines 5 years old and over.

Keep the salt compartment filled in hard water areas. The water softener in the machine protects spray arms, the element. And pump from scale; without salt, scale buildup shortens the machine's life much.

Every 6 months: vacuum the condenser coil at the back or underneath the fridge. A dusty condenser runs at higher temperature. Increasing electricity consumption by 5 to 15 percent and shortening compressor life.

Annually: test the door seal by closing the door on a piece of paper and pulling the paper out. It should offer clear resistance. A seal that allows paper to slide freely loses cold air and makes the compressor run more often.

attrs.symptom: Washing machine smells of mildew even after a hot wash

content.text: The mould is in the drum seal folds and in the internal pipework. Run three consecutive 90 degree cycles: one with a cup of bicarbonate of soda. One with 250 ml of white vinegar. And one empty rinse. After each wash, leave the door and detergent drawer open to allow the drum to dry. Going forward. Run at least one 60 degree wash per week: frequent 30 to 40 degree washes do not reach the temperature needed to kill mould in the drum.

attrs.symptom: Fridge is louder than usual and warmer inside

content.text: The compressor is working harder than normal. First check the condenser coil for dust: a heavily dusty coil explains both symptoms in the majority of cases. If cleaning the coil does not resolve the noise within 24 hours. The refrigerant circuit may have a slow leak or the compressor is failing. At that point. Get a repair estimate: if the estimate exceeds 50 percent of the cost of a similar replacement. Replacement is the better financial and environmental decision unless the machine is fewer than 5 years old.
Step-by-step instructions for Maintenance routines that extend domestic appliance lifespan go here.
